summary: Iteration 3 — Comprehensive E2E review of new Retailer Sales Book module. Backend: 9/9 pytest passing (all 6 endpoints validated: create, list, summary, analytics, mark-paid, CSV export). Frontend: sidebar role-gating verified (Sales Book nav only visible for retailer, hidden for manufacturer & distributor), /sales route renders with 3 tabs, POS dialog opens with product search → add to cart → qty inc/dec → payment toggle → submit closes with success toast; sale was actually persisted (analytics shows 5 tx · ₦25,300 · 30d). AI insights ribbon renders 5 well-tagged cards (critical/positive/warning/info) in Analytics tab.

minor.issue: Inventory deduction is not atomic across documents — between validate-loop and deduct-loop, a concurrent sale on the same line could over-sell. Acceptable for single-cashier POS but flag for multi-cashier scaling. Consider a single $inc update with a conditional filter (quantity: { $gte: requested }) per line for true atomicity.
Quantity validation accepts non-integers in Pydantic (quantity: int will coerce floats); negative validated via 'quantity <= 0' guard. Fine.

date_from / date_to are compared as string prefixes against ISO created_at — works because ISO 8601 sorts lexically, but date_from='2026-01-15' will only match sales whose created_at >= '2026-01-15' (i.e. midnight onwards). date_to gets '+T23:59:59Z' suffix which is correct. Document this in API or use proper datetime parsing.

issues: AI insights ribbon footer label hard-codes 'Powered by Claude Haiku' — fine if accurate but verify the actual model used by services.ai_insights matches.
Unit-price input is editable per line (could let an attendant accidentally undercharge); consider read-only price with explicit 'Discount' field instead.

action_items: Optional: tighten inventory deduction to a single conditional $inc per line for true concurrency safety.
Optional: replace the dict-loop with $inc retry-on-fail pattern if multi-cashier is on roadmap.
Optional: re-evaluate whether attendant should be able to edit unit_price in the POS dialog (risk of mispricing).
critical_code_review_comments: routes/sales.py is 444 lines — within the 700-line guideline; analytics computation is in-memory in Python (acceptable up to ~20k sales/window).
SalesBookView.jsx is 987 lines — exceeds the 700-line guideline. Recommend splitting into DashboardTab.jsx, LedgerTab.jsx, AnalyticsTab.jsx, SaleEntryDialog.jsx for maintainability.
create_sale validates stock, then deducts in a second loop without a transaction; two concurrent sales of the last unit could both pass validation. Use conditional $inc per line.

daily_sales sync happens per line item inside the second loop — if line N fails after lines 1..N-1 succeeded, the sales doc won't exist but daily_sales will have stranded rows. Wrap in try/except + rollback if needed.
mark_sale_paid does NOT push a notification — consider adding for symmetry with create_sale credit path.

Unit price is taken from the request body (payload) rather than the product catalog — opens door to discrepancies between displayed price and recorded sale. Verify product.unit_price on the server or accept as the cashier's intent (current behavior).

success_rate.backend: 100% (9/9 pytest, plus pre-existing 24 supply-chain tests untouched)

test_credentials: None required — role switcher landing page.
seed_data_creation: Existing seed used (POST /api/seed idempotent). Sales were created during the test run on the FIRST retailer (Best Bazaar / Lagos · Ikorodu). Three TEST_ tagged sales added (2x cash @ ₦2000, credit @ ₦500 marked paid, plus E2E_Tester sale via UI).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test file: /app/backend/tests/test_sales.py. Uses first retailer from /api/retailers and any stocked inventory row. CSV is returned as streaming text/csv with proper headers. Frontend verified at viewport 1440x900. UI testids consolidated in SalesBookView.jsx: sales-book-view, sales-tab-{dashboard,ledger,analytics}, new-sale-btn, new-sale-fab, sale-entry-dialog, sale-product-search, product-pick-{id}, cart-items, qty-{inc,dec,input}-{id}, payment-{cash,transfer,pos,credit}, sale-customer, sale-attendant, sale-notes, sale-submit, ledger-{search,date-from,date-to,payment-filter,export-csv,table,row,prev,next}.
rca_of_the_issue: No bugs found. All 14 requirements in the review request are satisfied. Two minor architectural notes (non-atomic deduction + over-long view file) are documented as future improvements, not blocking issues.
